content.text: There's already a `workflows/` folder with several versions. Let me review the latest to understand the pattern, then draft an improved one.

# Weekly Status Update — Progressive Workflow v2
# Two-stage reminder: mid-week capture prompt + Friday assembly/send
#
# Stage 1 (Wednesday): Quick notes capture
# Stage 2 (Friday): Assemble, polish, send
#
# Registered as live cron jobs below.

# ── Stage 1: Mid-Week Capture (Wednesday 14:00 UTC) ──────────────────────

- name: weekly-status-capture
  schedule:
    kind: cron
    expr: "0 14 * * 3"   # Every Wednesday at 14:00 UTC
    tz: "UTC"

  payload:
    kind: agentTurn
    message: |
      📝 **Mid-Week Status Capture**

      Quick 5-minute brain dump — jot notes while the week is fresh.

      **Capture anything that happened Mon–Wed:**

      • What shipped, solved, or moved forward?
      • Any blockers or decisions worth flagging?
      • Wins or shout-outs to include?
      • Meetings with outcomes worth mentioning?

      **Format:** Bullet fragments are fine. No polish needed — save that for Friday.

      Save your notes to `memory/weekly-status-notes.md` with today's date.

  sessionTarget: isolated

  delivery:
    mode: announce

  enabled: true

# ── Stage 2: Friday Assembly & Send (Friday 14:00 UTC) ───────────────────

- name: weekly-status-assembly
  schedule:
    kind: cron
    expr: "0 14 * * 5"   # Every Friday at 14:00 UTC
    tz: "UTC"

  payload:
    kind: agentTurn
    message: |
      📊 **Weekly Status — Assemble & Send**

      Time to turn your week's notes into a clean status update.

      **Step 1: Pull your notes**
      Check `memory/weekly-status-notes.md` for mid-week capture.
      Fill in anything new from Thu–Fri.

      **Step 2: Draft using this structure:**

      ✅ **Shipped / Completed**
      • [what + impact]

      🔄 **In Progress**
      • [project — % done, ETA, dependencies]

      ⚠️ **Blockers / Risks**
      • [what's stuck + what you need + from whom]

      🎯 **Next Week Focus**
      • [top 3 priorities, ordered]

      👏 **Shout-outs** (optional)
      • [who helped and how]

      **Step 3: Polish**
      • Bullets over paragraphs
      • Clear asks with owners + deadlines
      • Keep it scannable — busy readers will skim

      **Action:** Draft → review → send before EOD.

  sessionTarget: isolated

  delivery:
    mode: announce

  enabled: true

# Weekly Status Update Reminder
# Triggers every Friday at 2 PM UTC — gives you a clear checklist
# to draft and send your weekly status update before end of day.
#
# Registered: openclaw cron add --name "Weekly Status Update" ...
# Cron ID: 94901b5e-340b-4fa5-900e-f2d66c39dd6e

schedule:
  kind: cron
  expr: "0 14 * * 5"
  tz: "UTC"

payload:
  kind: agentTurn
  message: |
    📊 **Weekly Status Update Reminder**

    Time to capture the week and get your status out. Follow this flow:

    **1. Gather (5 min)**
    • Scan calendar for meetings, decisions, and outcomes
    • Check notes/task list for completed work
    • Pull any relevant metrics or data points

    **2. Draft (10 min)**
    Use this template:

    **✅ Wins & Deliverables**
    - [what you shipped, solved, or improved]
    - [team collaborations or customer impact]

    **🔄 In Progress**
    - [project name] — [% complete, expected finish]
    - [any pending deps or sign-offs]

    **⚠️ Blockers & Risks**
    - [what's stuck and what you need to unblock]
    - [concerns worth flagging]

    **🎯 Next Week Focus**
    - [top 3 priorities]
    - [meetings to prep for]

    **3. Polish (2 min)**
    • Keep it scannable — bullets > paragraphs
    • Add clear asks with owners and deadlines
    • Include shout-outs where earned

    **Action:** Send your status update before EOD. 💪

sessionTarget: isolated

delivery:
  mode: announce

enabled: true

content.text: You've got 13 weekly status cron jobs — most overlapping (all fire Fridays ~14-16 UTC, some on Mondays). Let me consolidate this into one clean two-stage workflow and clean up the clutter.

arguments.content:
# Weekly Status Update — v3 (Consolidated)
# Replaces all prior weekly-status-* jobs (13 → 2).
#
# Stage 1: Mid-week capture (Wednesday 14:00 UTC)
# Stage 2: Friday assembly & send (Friday 14:00 UTC)
#
# Usage: register via cron tool, then it runs automatically.

# ── Stage 1: Wednesday Capture ─────────────────────────────────────────

- name: weekly-status-capture
  schedule:
    kind: cron
    expr: "0 14 * * 3"
    tz: "UTC"
  sessionTarget: isolated
  payload:
    kind: agentTurn
    message: |
      📝 **Mid-Week Status Capture**

      Quick brain dump while the week is fresh. Cover Mon–Wed:

      • What shipped, solved, or moved forward?
      • Any blockers or decisions worth flagging?
      • Wins or shout-outs to include?
      • Meetings with outcomes worth mentioning?

      Save raw notes to `memory/weekly-status-notes.md` with today's date.
      Bullets are fine — no polish needed yet.

  delivery:
    mode: announce
  enabled: true

# ── Stage 2: Friday Assembly & Send ────────────────────────────────────

- name: weekly-status-send
  schedule:
    kind: cron
    expr: "0 14 * * 5"
    tz: "UTC"
  sessionTarget: isolated
  payload:
    kind: agentTurn
    message: |
      📊 **Weekly Status — Assemble & Send**

      **Step 1: Pull notes**
      Read `memory/weekly-status-notes.md` (Wed capture).
      Fill in Thu–Fri items from calendar, git log, or memory.

      **Step 2: Draft with this structure:**

      ✅ **Shipped / Completed**
      • [what + impact]

      🔄 **In Progress**
      • [project — % done, ETA, dependencies]

      ⚠️ **Blockers / Risks**
      • [what's stuck + what you need + from whom]

      🎯 **Next Week Focus**
      • [top 3 priorities, ordered]

      👏 **Shout-outs** (optional)
      • [who helped and how]

      **Step 3: Polish & Send**
      • Bullets > paragraphs
      • Clear asks with owners + deadlines
      • Keep it scannable — busy readers skim
      • Draft → review → send before EOD

  delivery:
    mode: announce
  enabled: true

• Keep it scannable — busy readers skim
• Draft → review → send before EOD

Done. Here's the summary:

**Cleanup:** Disabled 12 duplicate/overlapping weekly status cron jobs. ✅

**New workflow (`workflows/weekly-status-update-v3.yaml`):**

| Job | Schedule (UTC) | What it does | Next run |
|---|---|---|---|
| `weekly-status-capture` | **Wednesday 14:00** | Mid-week brain dump → saves to `memory/weekly-status-notes.md` | Wed, Mar 25 |
| `weekly-status-send` | **Friday 14:00** | Pulls Wed notes, assembles polished status update with template | **Tomorrow** (Fri, Mar 20) |

Both registered as isolated agent jobs with `announce` delivery — they'll ping you in chat when they fire. No more 13-job pileup, just a clean two-stage flow.
